subject: Ecology
title: What Is Co-Evolution?
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: What Is Co-Evolution?.

hook.text: When a flower shapes a bee and the bee shapes the flower back, that's co-evolution.

buildup.text: Over millions of years, pollinators and plants evolve in tandem—each adapting to the other.

discovery.text: Longer tongues access deeper nectar; deeper tubes exclude shorter-tongued competitors.

twist.text: Co-evolution can lock species into mutual dependence—lose one and the other follows.

climax.text: Pollination networks show that co-evolution builds the structure of entire ecosystems.

quiz.question: What risk does tight co-evolution create?
options: Mutual dependence—losing one species threatens the other
